published.text:
### Hero
Heading: n8n vs Zapier for Small Business
Body: Both tools automate workflows. But they solve different problems at different price points. This page helps you figure out which one fits your business.
Sub: No affiliate links. No vendor sponsorship. Just a practical breakdown based on building with both tools for real businesses.

### New/updated decision-routing section
Heading: Three real scenarios, three different answers
Intro: Most small businesses fall into one of these situations. Here is what the decision looks like for each:

1. "I just need form submissions to go into my CRM and trigger a follow-up email"
- Frames this as a straightforward SaaS-to-SaaS workflow where Zapier is usually enough.
- Adds a natural next-step link to /n8n-automation-examples-small-business for readers whose simple automation is starting to grow into something bigger.

2. "I need leads routed differently based on source, service type, or location"
- Frames this as the point where branching logic, API lookups, and conditional routing push the reader toward n8n.
- Adds contextual internal links to /ai-lead-follow-up-for-service-businesses and /n8n-consultant-vs-diy-small-business.

3. "I inherited a mess of Zaps or n8n workflows and nothing is documented"
- Reframes the platform choice around audit-before-migration instead of guessing.
- Adds contextual internal links to /n8n-workflow-audit-checklist, /n8n-workflow-cleanup-vs-rebuild, and /n8n-workflow-cleanup-service.

### Updated related-links area
Section: See how n8n works in practice
- Keeps the two case-study cards.
- Expands the bottom link row beyond /case-studies to include:
  - /n8n-automation-consultant
  - /how-to-hire-n8n-automation-consultant
  - /n8n-automation-consultant-cost-small-business

### Rest of page retained
- The short version
- What each tool actually is
- Side-by-side comparison
- The cost question
- When this decision actually matters
- Who should use which
- Common questions
- Final CTA and booking widget

edits.description: This was an indexing/internal-linking refresh on an existing comparison page, not a new page ship.
The added section keeps the page in decision mode by showing three practical small-business situations instead of adding generic tool education.
Internal links stay tightly inside the live n8n cluster so the page routes readers toward examples, consultant help, hiring guidance, pricing, and rescue paths without drifting into unrelated topics.

Tone stays direct and neutral: Zapier is still presented as a valid answer for simple workflows, while n8n is positioned as the better fit only when complexity, cost scale, or ownership needs justify it.
No new proof claims were invented. The update only strengthens framing and crawl paths using already-live site assets.
